title: The Thursday Review
dream_body: I was presenting my Thursday review to a small room of people I already knew. My slides were prepared. Midway through, one chart had the wrong month labeled on it. I noticed, corrected the label out loud, and continued. Nobody mocked me. I did not feel panicked — just briefly alert, then steady again. Afterward someone asked a practical question I could answer. The review ended on time. I woke feeling ordinary, not shaken.

Respond with Adaptive Insight V2 JSON for this single dream only.
Primary goal: help the dreamer recognize something meaningful they had not already typed.
Default to empty threads and empty questions. Add optional sections only when they deepen the central recognition from a distinct angle.
If the dream is genuinely rich, do not strip earned Threads merely to stay short — and do not overcompress the notice.
Choose depth by evidence, not length. Prefer earned recognition over summary. Prefer restraint over invention.

Title: The Thursday Review
Dream:
I was presenting my Thursday review to a small room of people I already knew. My slides were prepared. Midway through, one chart had the wrong month labeled on it. I noticed, corrected the label out loud, and continued. Nobody mocked me. I did not feel panicked — just briefly alert, then steady again. Afterward someone asked a practical question I could answer. The review ended on time. I woke feeling ordinary, not shaken.
raw_content: {"version":2,"depth":"focused","notice":"The important part may be that the mistake never becomes a crisis. You catch it, name it plainly, and keep going; the room accepts the correction, and your competence remains intact. Even the ending reinforces this: you can still answer what is asked, finish on time, and leave the review as an ordinary event. One possible reading is that your mind is rehearsing a steadier standard for being capable— not never making an error, but recovering without shame or loss of standing.","threads":[],"reflection_questions":[],"confidence":"high","pattern_candidates":[{"type":"action","label":"Calm repair after a visible mistake","evidence":"You corrected the mislabeled month out loud and continued the presentation.","confidence":"explicit"},{"type":"emotion","label":"Alertness returning to steadiness","evidence":"You were briefly alert rather than panicked, then became steady again.","confidence":"explicit"},{"type":"relationship","label":"Acceptance despite imperfection","evidence":"The familiar audience did not mock you, and the interaction remained practical.","confidence":"explicit"}]}

insight.notice: The important part may be that the mistake never becomes a crisis. You catch it, name it plainly, and keep going; the room accepts the correction, and your competence remains intact. Even the ending reinforces this: you can still answer what is asked, finish on time, and leave the review as an ordinary event. One possible reading is that your mind is rehearsing a steadier standard for being capable— not never making an error, but recovering without shame or loss of standing.

pattern_candidates.label: Alertness returning to steadiness
pattern_candidates.evidence: You were briefly alert rather than panicked, then became steady again.

pattern_candidates.label: Acceptance despite imperfection
pattern_candidates.evidence: The familiar audience did not mock you, and the interaction remained practical.
